div.h1: Why cd Must Be a Builtin
div.p: Child processes cannot modify parent state — chdir() only affects the calling process
Problem: Child's chdir() changed child's cwd, but parent's cwd remains /home/user. Shell's working directory is unchanged!
Success: chdir() called directly in shell process. Kernel updates the shell's cwd — change persists!
chdir() only modifies current->fs->pwd. Parent and child have separate fs_struct after fork (copy-on-write).

ul.li: Child gets its own cwd, environ, file descriptors
Changes in child never propagate to parent
cd, export, unset MUST be builtins This is fundamental Unix process isolation.
